exercises.title: Inspect Process Maps
exercises.description: Open your own process memory map and identify text, heap, stack, and mmap regions.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can point to the major virtual memory regions in the output.

exercises.title: Read Memory Pressure
exercises.description: Run free and vmstat to understand system memory usage and swap activity.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can explain the major columns and whether the machine is under memory pressure.

exercises.title: Sequential vs Random Access
exercises.description: Write a small benchmark that walks an array sequentially and randomly, then compare timings.
exercises.expected_outcome: Sequential access should be measurably faster than random access on the same data size.

exercises.title: Detect a Memory Leak
exercises.description: Create a small leak in a toy program and detect it with valgrind or heaptrack.
exercises.expected_outcome: The tool should report leaked allocations or still-reachable blocks.

exercises.title: Pointer-Chasing Latency Test
exercises.description: Build a pointer-chasing benchmark that explores cache and RAM latency at different working-set sizes.
exercises.expected_outcome: Latency should jump as the working set moves beyond cache sizes and into RAM.

exercises.title: Simple Allocator
exercises.description: Implement a basic malloc/free wrapper with mmap, splitting, and coalescing.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can allocate and free blocks while observing reuse of freed memory.
